# Role Cards Template v0.2

**Purpose:** One-page role cards for officials, volunteers, and event leads.
**Use:** Generated by the Competition Planning Copilot after staffing is known.

## Role Card Fields

Each role card must include:

- Role name
- Role owner
- Arrival time
- Station / location
- Primary responsibilities
- What this role may decide
- What this role must escalate
- Equipment needed
- Communication channel
- Start-gate relevance
- Emergency escalation
- End-of-event handoff

## Competition Director

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Owns event go/no-go, escalation, and final operational decisions. |
| May decide | Operational sequencing, staffing redeployment, delay/cancel recommendations after consulting relevant leads. |
| Must escalate | Safety, safeguarding, medical, venue security, major reputational issues. |
| Equipment | Master timetable, radio/phone, contact sheet, incident forms, start-gate checklist. |
| Start-gate relevance | Final go/no-go owner. |

## Technical Lead

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Owns technical competition format, timetable approval, event sequencing, and officials briefing. |
| May decide | Technical timetable edits, event sequencing, heat/order changes inside approved rules. |
| Must escalate | Eligibility/seeding disputes, unsafe technical conditions, unresolved rules issues. |
| Equipment | Rules reference, timetable, event sheets, hurdle/implement specs, radio/phone. |
| Start-gate relevance | Final timetable, timing method, and technical officials. |

## Call Room / Athlete Flow Lead

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Gets athletes to the right event at the right time without congestion. |
| May decide | Queue management, athlete movement timing, volunteer placement inside call-room zone. |
| Must escalate | Missing athlete, athlete distress, conflicting events, eligibility concerns. |
| Equipment | Call sheets, timetable, radio/phone, pens, late-athlete log. |
| Start-gate relevance | Call-room lead must be assigned before event starts. |

## Results Lead

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Owns results capture, verification, correction log, and publication readiness. |
| May decide | Data entry workflow, verification sequence, results correction workflow before publication. |
| Must escalate | Disputed results, timing failure, missing field sheets, publication errors. |
| Equipment | Laptop, results sheets, backup paper forms, printer if available, power, Wi-Fi/hotspot. |
| Start-gate relevance | Results verification owner must be assigned. |

## Field Event Lead

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Owns setup and flow for jumps/throws field events. |
| May decide | Attempt order, field crew positioning, measurement workflow within approved rules. |
| Must escalate | Safety sector issue, equipment mismatch, athlete double-booking, rule ambiguity. |
| Equipment | Event sheets, measuring equipment, markers, implements list, radio/phone. |
| Start-gate relevance | Critical for field-heavy events. |

## Medical Lead

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Owns medical readiness and medical response. |
| May decide | Medical treatment, athlete removal/return recommendations according to policy. |
| Must escalate | Serious incident, ambulance need, heat illness, safeguarding concern. |
| Equipment | First-aid supplies, ice/water, incident form, emergency contacts. |
| Start-gate relevance | Event cannot start without medical cover. |

## Safeguarding Lead

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Owns safeguarding presence and escalation. |
| May decide | Safeguarding escalation path and protective actions according to policy. |
| Must escalate | Any safeguarding concern or disclosure. |
| Equipment | Safeguarding policy, incident form, private conversation route, contact sheet. |
| Start-gate relevance | Event cannot start without safeguarding lead. |

## Communications Lead

| Field | Content |
|---|---|
| Primary responsibility | Owns approved communications and tone. |
| May decide | Draft wording, channel order, timing of approved updates. |
| Must escalate | Public statement, crisis message, timetable publication, results publication. |
| Equipment | Approved messages, contact groups, final timetable, approval log. |
| Start-gate relevance | External communications approval required before send/publish. |
